_comment: Default values for user-editable Bot Config settings AND server-internal operator-tunable defaults. The dashboard reads the top-level keys at init via GET /api/bot-config-defaults and uses them as the initial input value when .bot-config.json has no explicit override. The server also falls back to these when `getConfig` is asked for a value the user hasn't set. Top-level keys match the bot-config-v2 POSITION_KEYS / GLOBAL_KEYS naming so the same string flows from this file → dashboard input → .bot-config.json → bot reader. Nested groups (lowGasThresholds, residualCleanup) are server-internal: not exposed in the Bot Settings UI, consumed directly by src/gas-monitor.js and src/bot-cycle-residual.js. ui-defaults.json, dust-threshold.json and nft-providers.json stay standalone (different concern + data shape).
_priceCacheTtl_comment: Price-source cache TTL in milliseconds (in-memory cache inside src/price-fetcher.js, gated by the idle-driven pause). Default 120_000 (2 min) — long enough to absorb the 60 s poll cadence but short enough that a real price move shows up promptly when something's looking. The dust-unit-price cache TTL is computed at module-load as priceCacheTtlMs * dustUnitPriceCacheMultiplier, so the integer-multiple constraint is structural (a runtime assertion guards against future hand-edits).
_pricePauseExceptionPollWindowMultiple_c: Balanced-band Telegram notifier: number of bot poll cycles between fresh-price fetches that bypass the idle-driven price-lookup pause. Effective cadence = checkIntervalSec × pricePauseExceptionPollWindowMultiple seconds. Default 10 (10× poll = 10 min at the default 60 s poll). Higher = lighter price-source load, slower band-crossing detection. Positive integer >= 1. Only consulted when the 'positionBalanced' Telegram event is enabled.

lowGasThresholds._comment: Wallet low-gas detection tunables. Used by src/gas-monitor.js to compute two tiers for the Mission Control 'Gas Running Low' / 'Gas Critical' badge and matching Telegram alerts. Formula (chain-agnostic — gas opcodes are identical across EVMs): floor_gas_units = standardSendGas × worstCaseGasFactor (one worst-case rebalance); recommended_gas_units = floor_gas_units × safetyMultiplier × managedPositionCount. Native-token threshold = gas_units × live gasPrice. Tiers: 'ok' at balance ≥ recommended; 'low' at floor ≤ balance < recommended (amber static); 'critical' at balance < floor (blinking — next rebalance will likely fail with out-of-gas). worstCaseGasFactor=91 was derived from observed PulseChain rebalance_log.json + live logs (NFT #158965→158967) covering a rebalance that fired all 3 corrective-swap iterations: remove(~209k) + primary approve(~46k) + primary swap(~243k) + 3×corrective(approve+swap ~290k each = ~870k) + mint approves(~76k) + mint(~464k) ≈ 1,911,000 gas / 21,000 send ≈ 91. safetyMultiplier=3 is the user-specified headroom factor. standardSendGas=21000 is the EVM constant for a native-token transfer with no calldata. If the JSON is missing or malformed, gas-monitor falls back to these same defaults so the guard never silently disables.

residualCleanup._comment: Automatic residual-cleanup rebalance tunables. After every successful normal or manual rebalance, if the wallet residual share of the pool batch (residual / (LP + residual)) still exceeds thresholdPct past delayMs, the bot fires exactly one cleanup rebalance to sweep residuals back into the position. The cleanup respects ALL other rebalance rules (throttle, daily cap, doubling, swap-backoff, pause). Back-to-back cleanups are prevented by a per-position flag that only clears on the next normal rebalance. Not exposed in Bot Settings — operator-tunable via this file only.
